mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates an improving labor market, which typically boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with a 3-month decline in UNRATE over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month annualized growth rate of retail sales (RSAFS) exceeds 5%, it signals robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An environment characterized by a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS below 0.25%) combined with a recovering industrial production (INDPRO 3-month growth above 0.5%) indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting a nascent economic recovery, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are flat or negative in more than 60% of the periods where both trigger conditions are simultaneously met over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment (UNRATE above 8%), a sustained increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 0.1%) signals a turning point in the labor market, which typically precedes broader economic recovery and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE consistently remains above 8%, positive PAYEMS growth does not lead to statistically significant positive S&P 500 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) growth rate indicates a low inflation environment, allowing for sustained accommodative monetary policy. This supports higher equity valuations by reducing discount rates and increasing real earnings growth expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The S&P 500's 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with periods of CPIAUCSL annualized growth below 2% over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) to below 500 thousand units (3-month average) indicates a weakening housing market and broader economic slowdown. This negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the negative signal over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) is declining (3-month growth < -0.5%) and retail sales (RSAFS) are also weak (3-month growth < -0.5%), it signals a broad-based economic contraction affecting both supply and demand. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of the periods where both trigger conditions are simultaneously met over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery period, a strong rebound in GDP (GDPC1) growth (quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 3%) signals a robust economic recovery. This typically leads to a re-rating of equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: During identified post-recession recovery periods, strong GDPC1 growth does not consistently lead to statistically significant positive S&P 500 60-day returns.
